title: Sequential Detergent and Salt-based Subcellular Fractionation of Human Neural Stem Cells
author: Katja Piltti

steps.title: Detach the cells using a cell...
steps.instruction: Detach the cells using a cell scraper in 500 μL PBS (0.1 mM Ca²⁺ and 1 mM Mg²⁺) supplemented with protease inhibitor (Roche Applied Science, cOmplete ULTRA, 05892970001) and phosphatase inhibitor (Roche Applied Science, PhosSTOP, 04906837001). Keep on ice to prevent protein degradation.

steps.title: Homogenize the detached cells by passing...
steps.instruction: Homogenize the detached cells by passing them through a 1 mL syringe fitted with a 27.5 G needle, using 10 up-and-down strokes. This disrupts the plasma membrane, releasing cytoplasmic contents.

steps.title: Incubate the homogenate on ice for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the homogenate on ice for 10 minutes to ensure complete lysis.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C. Ensure the centrifuge is pre-cooled.

steps.title: Carefully collect the supernatant as the PBS-soluble cytoplasmic protein fraction
steps.instruction: Carefully collect the supernatant as the PBS-soluble cytoplasmic protein fraction.

steps.title: Resuspend the remaining cell pellet in...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the remaining cell pellet in 500μL of lysis buffer containing 50mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.5), 100mM NaCl, 0.5% Triton X-100, and protease and phosphatase inhibitors. The Triton X-100 helps solubilize membrane proteins.

steps.title: Use a 1 mL syringe fitted...
steps.instruction: Use a 1 mL syringe fitted with a 35 G needle for further disruption, performing 10 additional up-and-down strokes.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 10 minutes...
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 10 minutes followed by centrifugation at 12,000 g for 15 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Collect the supernatant as the detergent...
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatant as the detergent (TX-100)-soluble membrane/subcellular structure protein fraction.

steps.title: Resuspend the remaining pellet in 200μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the remaining pellet in 200μL of lysis buffer with 0.5M KCl and protease and phosphatase inhibitors to extract nuclear proteins.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 10 minutes...
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 10 minutes to allow salt to permeabilize the nuclear envelope.

steps.title: Centrifuge again at 12,000 g for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge again at 12,000 g for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Collect the supernatant as the TX-100+ KCl-nuclear protein fraction
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatant as the TX-100+ KCl-nuclear protein fraction.

steps.title: Resuspend the final pellet in 20μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the final pellet in 20μL of 5X SDS-PAGE sample loading buffer (250mM Tris-HCl, pH 6.8, 10% SDS, 30% glycerol, 10mM DTT, 0.05% bromophenol blue).

steps.title: Boil the sample for 5 minutes...
steps.instruction: Boil the sample for 5 minutes to denature proteins, preparing them for analysis.

steps.title: Analyze 10μg of protein from each...
steps.instruction: Analyze 10μg of protein from each fraction using Western blot employing antibodies specific to compartment markers, and loading controls.

steps.title: Wash hNSC twice with cold Dulbecco's...
steps.instruction: Wash hNSC twice with cold Dulbecco's Phosphate-Buffered Saline without Ca 2+ /Mg 2+ (DPBS -/-).

steps.title: Culture hNSC on poly-L-ornithine and laminin-coated...
steps.instruction: Culture hNSC on poly-L-ornithine and laminin-coated 6-well plates in growth medium (GM).
